name: ML Recommendation and Constrained Filtering
scope_implemented: RecommendationEngine (src/ml/recommendation.py): ML Stage 3 — converts ShadowBundles into structured MLRecommendation objects; never auto-applied; all entries logged to shadow_logs with mode='RECOMMEND' and applied=False

MLFilter (src/ml/filter.py): ML Stage 4 Constrained Filter — sits between deterministic candidate generator and risk manager; may BLOCK weak candidates; enforces 5 safety invariants: output_len ≤ input_len, output ⊆ input, hard blockers never unblocked, strategy_enabled=False always blocked, candidates are frozen so no field mutation possible
MLConfig extended (src/ml/config.py): RecommendationCfg and FilterCfg dataclasses added; load_ml_config() parses recommendation/filter YAML sections
configs/ml.yaml advanced to ml_stage=4 (CONSTRAINED_FILTER) with recommendation.enabled=true and filter.enabled=true, min_confidence_to_take=0.40

Phase 10 gate checks (src/gates/phase10.py): 8 additional ML-PROMO criteria — Stage 3/4 imports, recommendation produces output, applied=False invariant, DB applied=False, filter blocks candidates, filter cannot create candidates, no risk increase (frozen dataclass), hard-blocked strategy blocked regardless of ML confidence
ML-PROMO gate updated (src/gates/checks.py): combined check_ml_promo() = Phase 9 checks + check_ml_phase10(); single gate entry point now covers all 20 criteria
src/ml/__init__.py: FilterDecision, FilterResult, MLFilter, MLRecommendation, RecommendationEngine, RecommendationRunResult exported

Test suite (tests/test_phase10_ml_filter.py): 28 new tests covering config parsing, RecommendationEngine invariants, MLFilter safety properties, hard-blocker enforcement, FilterResult helpers, invalid threshold validation

acceptance_criteria.criterion: ML may block candidates / recommend; cannot create trades, raise risk, or override risk manager
acceptance_criteria.evidence: filter_no_create PASS (passed 10 ≤ input 10; all passed are original candidates); filter_no_risk_increase PASS (passed candidates are identical Python objects — no field mutation, Candidate is frozen=True); filter_hard_blocked PASS (strategy_disabled always blocked regardless of ML confidence)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: ML Stage 3 recommendations shown in dashboard/reports; applied=False always
acceptance_criteria.evidence: recommendation_mode PASS (13 recommendations produced; all applied=False); recommendation_no_live_influence PASS (0 Stage-3 recommendations have applied=True); recommendation_db_applied_false PASS (all RECOMMEND shadow_log entries have applied=False)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: ML Stage 4 filter can block weak candidates
acceptance_criteria.evidence: filter_can_block PASS (filter blocked 7/10 candidates in gate test)
make -s run-gate GATE=ML-PROMO FORMAT=json → overall: PASS (20/20 criteria); report at reports/gates/ML-PROMO_20260617T204524.json

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Promotion only if ML-PROMO PASS; shadow validation passed
acceptance_criteria.evidence: ml_expectancy_improves PASS (model expectancy=0.6936R vs baseline=0.1254R, +0.5682R); ml_profit_factor_preserved PASS (PF=18.896 vs baseline 1.618); ml_tail_risk_reduced PASS (worst=-0.2686R vs baseline -0.7333R); ml_best_trades_preserved PASS (10% top trades removed ≤ 20% allowed)

ml_no_live_influence PASS (6900 SHADOW entries; 0 applied entries); RecommendationEngine always sets applied=False; MLFilter.apply() uses write_to_db logging only — no order placement; ml_stage=4 means CONSTRAINED_FILTER shadow mode, not live

criteria.detail: 100 shadow log entries written, applied=False on all
RecommendationEngine, MLFilter, and related types importable
RECOMMEND mode shadow_log entries: applied=False on all
passed 10 <= input 10; all passed are original candidates
passed candidates are identical Python objects — no field mutation
strategy_disabled candidate blocked even with high ML confidence; normal candidate passed

tests.ruff: All checks passed (ruff check src tests)
tests.mypy: Success: no issues found in 116 source files

assumptions_and_decisions: Stage 3 (RecommendationEngine) reuses existing shadow_logs table with mode='RECOMMEND' and applied=False — no new migration needed; this matches Phase 9 ShadowLog schema exactly
Stage 4 (MLFilter) reuses shadow_logs with mode='CONSTRAINED_FILTER' and applied=True — still shadow-level logging, not live order placement
Frozen Candidate dataclass (@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)) is the structural enforcement of 'filter cannot modify candidates' — no additional runtime check needed beyond identity assertions
RiskBucket type is float (not Literal[1.0, 0.5, 0.25, 0.0]) because mypy does not support float Literals; the constraint is enforced by the _risk_bucket() method logic (never returns > 1.0)
ml_stage advancing from 2 to 4 requires test_ml_config_loads in Phase 9 test suite to check >= 2 instead of == 2; this is correct progression per Section 20
check_ml_promo() in checks.py combines Phase 9 + Phase 10 criteria into a single gate function — single entry point in CHECKS registry, no gate split needed
ML-PROMO gate runs 20/20 criteria combining shadow validation metrics from Phase 9 and Stage 3/4 safety invariants from Phase 10
known_gaps: RecommendationEngine does not yet feed into the dashboard UI (Phase 9 dashboard shows shadow stats; Stage 3 recommendation panel is future UI work)
MLFilter is wired as a standalone apply() call; integration with the live candidate pipeline routing is Phase 13+ scope
min_confidence_to_take=0.40 default is conservatively set; calibration against live paper trading data would refine this threshold
tested: yes — 344/344 tests pass; ruff + mypy clean; ML-PROMO gate PASS (20/20 criteria)
